20 workflows found (13 active). No specific Daily Wage Calculator triggers. navigation_map_coverage.label: Payroll Settings
elements_found: Payroll settings page with 16 accordion sections
Configure salaries section with Manage button

primary_entity.name: Daily Wage Calculation Service
primary_entity.identified_from: The Daily Wage Calculation section contains a table of 3 service rows (Salary proration, EOS leave encashment, Unpaid leave deduction). Each row represents a configuration for how daily wage is calculated for that service type. There is no primary entity to create/delete - these are fixed configuration items that can only be edited.

own_logic_used: Checked for Add/Create buttons - none found. The 3 services are fixed rows.
create.evidence: No create functionality exists. The Daily Wage Calculation section contains exactly 3 fixed service rows that cannot be added to or removed.
create.notes: This is a configuration feature, not a CRUD entity feature.

payload_triggers_used: Viewed table rows in the expanded Daily Wage Calculation section
own_logic_used: Clicked edit pencil icons to see full configuration details for each service
read.evidence: doc-02-daily-wage-calc-section.png - Table shows Service name and current Daily Wage Calculation formula for each row.
read.notes: Current values visible: Salary proration = Basic salary + allowances / 30 days, EOS = Calculated in end of service eligibility, Unpaid leave = Calculated in leave policy

payload_triggers_used: Edit pencil icon on each row
own_logic_used: Tested all 3 dropdown options in Salary Proration dialog
update.evidence: doc-03-salary-proration-dialog.png, doc-04-dropdown-options.png, doc-05-working-days-formula.png, doc-06-calendar-days-formula.png - Edit dialog opens with Month calculation dropdown (Working days, Calendar days, Custom days). Formula updates dynamically. Cancel and Save buttons present.
update.notes: Did not save changes to avoid altering live configuration. All 3 edit dialogs functional.

own_logic_used: Checked for delete/trash icons - none found on any row
delete.evidence: No delete functionality exists. Service rows are fixed.
delete.notes: By design - the 3 services are system-defined and cannot be removed.

triggers_found: Leave salary request status is changed
actions_found: Create one time pay item (Bayzat Payroll)
workflow_integration.evidence: workflow-01-workflows-list.png, workflow-02-events-list.png - No specific 'Daily Wage Calculator' trigger exists among 65 total event triggers. Payroll-related triggers like 'Employee salary is updated' and 'Payroll cycle is closed' are indirectly related since daily wage settings affect salary calculations.

default_flow.approvers: Super Admin OR Payroll table admin
default_flow.note: Daily wage config changes save immediately without approval

what_to_do_results.task: Navigated to Settings > Payroll, expanded the Daily Wage Calculation accordion, and tested the Salary Proration edit dialog by opening it and cycling through all 3 month calculation options (Working days, Calendar days, Custom days).
actual_steps: Step 2: Clicked Payroll in Settings sub-menu
Step 3: Scrolled to Daily Wage Calculation section and clicked to expand accordion
Step 4: Viewed 3 service rows: Salary proration, EOS leave encashment, Unpaid leave deduction
Step 5: Clicked edit pencil on Salary proration row - dialog opened with Month calculation dropdown set to Custom days (30)
Step 6: Opened dropdown to see 3 options: Working days, Calendar days, Custom days
Step 7: Selected Working days - formula changed to (Basic salary + allowances) / Working days, Number of days field hidden
Step 8: Selected Calendar days - formula changed to (Basic salary + allowances) / Calendar days
Step 9: Clicked Cancel to avoid saving changes
what_to_do_results.result: Daily Wage Calculator configuration is accessible at Settings > Payroll > Daily Wage Calculation. The Salary Proration dialog allows selecting between Working days, Calendar days, or Custom days. Custom days allows specifying a number (currently 30). The formula updates dynamically based on selection. EOS leave encashment and Unpaid leave deduction rows link to their respective policy configurations.

what_to_do_results.payload_task: Calculate and Process Daily Pay Deductions for Unpaid Leave
what_to_do_results.task: Explored the Unpaid Leave Deduction dialog and the Payroll Table to verify unpaid leave deductions are processed. Observed the unpaid leave adjustments banner on the Payroll Table.
what_to_do_results.persona: HR Admin | People Manager | Finance Manager
actual_steps: Step 1: Clicked edit pencil on Unpaid leave deduction row in Daily Wage Calculation table
Step 2: Dialog opened showing 'Overwrite daily wage calculation in policies' toggle (off) and table of 13 unpaid leave types with their calculation methods
Step 3: Observed various calculation methods: most use '/ 30 days', some use '/ 30.41 days', one uses '/ Calendar days', one uses '/ 100 days'
Step 4: Closed dialog and navigated to Payroll > Payroll table
Step 5: Observed banner: '2 employees have unpaid leave adjustments' with 'Filter 2 Employees' link
Step 6: Noted current payroll cycle is January 2026 (01 Jan - 31 Jan 2026) with Open status
what_to_do_results.result: Unpaid leave deduction configuration is visible in the Daily Wage Calculation section. Each unpaid leave type has its own daily wage calculation method. The Payroll Table confirms that unpaid leave adjustments are automatically generated when leave requests are approved. However, the actual deduction calculation process happens automatically and cannot be manually tested without approving a leave request.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-001: EOS and Leave Encashment Calculation Limitations
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.payload_limitation: The platform cannot dynamically adjust End of Service (EOS) and leave encashment calculations for employees with mid-month departures and unpaid leave periods. The system rigidly calculates EOS using full monthly salary without considering actual days worked or payment received. Additionally, configuration changes in daily wage calculation are not immediately or clearly reflected in the EOS Eligibility section, creating confusion about whether settings have been successfully applied.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The EOS leave encashment and daily wage calculation settings are in separate accordion sections with no visible connection. The EOS dialog shows 47+ paid leave types with per-policy calculation methods. The Overwrite toggle is OFF, so per-policy settings override the global setting. The End of Service eligibility section shows only 1 leave type with a Configure button, disconnected from the EOS leave encashment dialog.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Opened EOS leave Encashment dialog - shows 47+ leave types with individual calculation methods (Calendar days or Working days). Opened End of service eligibility section - shows separate table with only 1 leave type and a Configure button. No visible link between these two sections.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-002: Lack of Configurable Salary Proration Methods
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.payload_limitation: The platform lacks granular salary proration configuration and applies a single rigid proration approach across all employees. Users cannot customize calculation methods between calendar days, working days, or group-specific approaches.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The Salary Proration dialog has only one global dropdown with 3 options (Working days, Calendar days, Custom days). No option exists for per-department, per-group, or per-employee calculation methods. The current setting (Custom days = 30) applies to all employees company-wide.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Opened Salary Proration dialog and tested all 3 dropdown options. Only one global Month calculation dropdown visible. No department/group selector or multi-rule configuration anywhere in the dialog.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-003: Daily Wage Divisor Precision and Leave Accrual Inconsistency
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.payload_limitation: The payroll system uses a custom daily wage divisor (30.42 days) instead of calendar days, which can result in deduction amounts that don't precisely match the monthly salary and creates user confusion about calculation methods.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Multiple divisors coexist in the Unpaid Leave Deduction dialog: most policies use / 30 days, Half Pay Leave and Maternity UAE and Sickdemo use / 30.41 days, Hospitalization uses / Calendar days, and one test policy uses / 100 days. This inconsistency within the same dialog could confuse administrators.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Opened Unpaid Leave Deduction dialog. Scrolled through all 13 leave types. Observed 4 different divisor methods in active use within the same table.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-004: Time Off and Payroll Module Synchronization Gap
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.payload_limitation: The Bayzat platform lacks real-time synchronization between Time Off and Payroll modules, preventing automatic recalculation when public holidays are added after leave requests or when leave request statuses change.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: The Payroll Table shows a banner confirming leave approvals trigger payroll adjustments ('2 employees have unpaid leave adjustments'). Could not test retroactive recalculation after public holiday changes without making data modifications. The synchronization appears one-directional based on UI observation.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Observed the unpaid leave adjustments banner on Payroll Table. The system does reflect leave approvals in payroll. Attempted to find retroactive recalculation controls but none visible. Could not test actual scenario without approving/modifying leave requests.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-005: Working Days Configuration Clarity and Documentation
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.payload_limitation: Users may experience confusion about how the working days configuration affects salary proration calculations. The lack of clear guidance on how work week configuration impacts monthly calculations can lead to support inquiries.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Selecting 'Working days' in the Salary Proration dialog shows the formula but provides no tooltip, help text, or documentation link explaining how the working days count is determined from the employee's work week configuration. The Number of days field disappears when Working days is selected, with no explanation of what value is used instead.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Selected 'Working days' in Salary Proration dialog. The formula shows '/ Working days' with no explanation. No tooltip on hover, no help icon, no 'Learn more' link. The Number of days field disappears completely when switching from Custom days to Working days.

summary: Daily Wage Calculator validation completed. The feature is a configuration-only tool at Settings > Payroll > Daily Wage Calculation with 3 fixed service rows (Salary proration, EOS leave encashment, Unpaid leave deduction). Salary Proration supports 3 calculation methods (Working days, Calendar days, Custom days) via a single global dropdown. EOS and Unpaid leave dialogs show per-policy calculation methods with an optional Overwrite toggle. No dedicated workflow triggers or approval flows exist. Approval Flows page checked - Payroll Transaction is the closest match. 3 of 5 WOF items confirmed, 1 not reproducible, 1 confirmed as by-design. Key gap: salary proration is a single global setting with no per-group customization.
